description: The exports map can block deep imports even when files exist on disk.
body: Use when import resolution fails for a dependency or local package. Do not bypass by reaching into dist internals unless the package intentionally supports it. Inspect exports/imports maps and choose a public entry or add an explicit export in the package owner. Verify with the same resolver: Node, bundler, or test runner. Risk is locking callers to private build layout. Use when JavaScript Runtime and Tooling work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
